mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at a cyclical peak (5.25%) represents restrictive monetary policy that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing below-average S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter regardless of short-term fluctuations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 5.25% for multiple consecutive months as of 2007-05-01
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return during periods with FEDFUNDS at or above its trailing 24-month 90th percentile is not significantly lower than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 20% signals a sharp contraction in residential construction demand that precedes broader consumer and credit stress, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as the market prices in slower growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value 1528 (Apr 2007) already down sharply from 2005-2006 peaks near 2000+
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following a >20% YoY HOUST decline are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth alongside a rising unemployment rate indicates a coincident turning point in the labor market that consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations have not yet priced in, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. Neither series alone is sufficient; the mechanism requires simultaneous weakening.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth below 1% AND UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4-4.5% near historic cycle lows as of May 2007
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are jointly met, 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from the sum of individual proxy effects.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in industrial production growth occurring during a late-cycle expansion signals diminishing supply-side momentum and impending margin compression for cyclical firms, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. In early-cycle or recovery regimes the same deceleration is noise rather than signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth falls below its trailing 3-year median
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 112.6889 (May 2007) reflecting late-expansion industrial activity
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, 60-day forward returns following an INDPRO growth deceleration are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows restricted to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week. This relationship is expected to hold continuously as an always-on trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 377885 million (May 2007) showing continued nominal retail sales growth
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive month-over-month RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than returns following negative RSAFS growth (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation rises above 2.5%, markets price in elevated risk of further Fed tightening or delayed easing, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 206.755 (May 2007) implying inflation running near or above 2.5% YoY
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with CPI YoY above 2.5% are not significantly lower than the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and a restrictive federal funds rate jointly amplify credit and demand contraction: tight monetary policy raises mortgage financing costs precisely as construction activity is already falling, producing a compounding negative effect on S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter that exceeds the sum of either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year decline exceeds 15% AND FEDFUNDS above 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% while HOUST vintage value 1528 down from prior cycle peak
regime_hint: monetary tightening combined with housing downturn
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both trigger conditions are jointly active are not significantly more negative than periods with only one condition met (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect fails to add incremental Rank IC beyond individual proxies.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate sits at or near a multi-decade cyclical low during a late-cycle expansion, the labor market has limited further room to tighten, and historical analogues show this condition precedes deteriorating forward equity returns as the market anticipates the next cyclical downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4-4.5%, near cycle lows as of May 2007
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with unemployment near cyclical lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods when UNRATE is within 0.3pp of its trailing 5-year low are not significantly lower than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows restricted to such periods.
